dsl_rule.translation_notes: Rounding bottom pattern characterized by: (1) slow accumulation phase represented by price action above medium-term MA (sma_50) showing gradual uptrend, (2) breakout from consolidation on relatively high volume (volume > hvol_20). Exit on trend line break below (close crosses below sma_50). Pattern parameters (62% success rate, 54% average rise) documented but not operationalized in rules. Throwback/pullback percentage not specified by author; stop loss set conservatively at 3%. Hold period extended to 180 days to accommodate multi-month pattern formation.

source_rule_text: Rounding bottom: A turn in trend characterized by slow, gradual price movement higher over months, forming a curved bottom pattern that signals a reversal to higher prices
